key_departures.impact: Robinson stepped up as WR1 after Malik Nabers' ACL tear, posting 92 catches, 1,014 yards, and 4 TDs in 2025. His departure leaves a significant hole as a reliable slot option and second receiver behind Nabers.
Flott had developed into a legitimate starter opposite Paulson Adebo, posting 11 passes defensed and 3 TFL in 2025. Losing him creates a meaningful CB2 hole in an already thin secondary.

Released as a cap casualty saving $9M. Okereke posted 143 tackles in 2025, fourth-most in franchise history, and was a two-year defensive captain. The cut directly created the need that Tremaine Edmunds was signed to fill.
Bellinger had declined to just 19 receptions in 2025 with Theo Johnson emerging. His departure was expected after the Isaiah Likely signing. Minimal impact on need picture.
Schlottmann provided versatile IOL depth, capable of filling in at both center and guard. His departure leaves the Giants without a reliable backup center under contract.

Belton was the team's special teams tackle leader and third safety who never carved out a full starting role. His departure slightly thins safety depth but doesn't create a starter-level hole given Nubin and Holland remain.
Released as a cap casualty saving $4.5M. Missed 2025 season with hip injury. Giants have Ben Sauls (8-for-8 FG as a rookie) as the in-house option and signed Jason Sanders as upgrade.
Released to make way for Jordan Stout. Gillan ranked 29th in punts inside the 20 and 30th in net average in 2025. Upgrade move, not a hole.
Kreiter had been the Giants' long-snapper for seven seasons. His departure leaves a hole at an often overlooked but critical specialist position.
Released as a cap casualty saving $5.38M. Hudson was a disaster when called upon in 2025. Depth loss only.

signings.analysis: 3yr/$40M ($47.5M upside with incentives) makes Likely the 3rd-highest-paid TE in the NFL. He reunites with Harbaugh and is the clear TE1 over Theo Johnson. Contract structure — 3 years, elite AAV for the position — signals a genuine starter investment. TE had been a moderate need; this fully resolves it with a young starter-caliber player. Giants will run heavy 12 personnel with Likely and Johnson. Note: Spotrac shows $0 in fully guaranteed money; likely structured as full-year guarantees. Guarantees TBD on exact structure, but AAV and years confirm starter commitment.

3yr/$36M with $23.7M guaranteed directly replaces Bobby Okereke at the green-dot ILB role. Edmunds posted 112 tackles, 4 INTs, and 9 PDs in 2025 before injury. At $12M/yr with significant guarantees (65%+ of total), this is a genuine starter investment. Giants also re-signed McFadden at $3.75M/yr as his partner. The duo is not a long-term answer (both on shorter deals), so LB may still be addressed in mid-rounds of the draft, but the immediate starting need is resolved. Need drops from tier 1 critical to tier 3.

Re-signing Eluemunor on a 3yr/$39M deal with $25.45M guaranteed locks up the starting RT opposite Andrew Thomas. Eluemunor graded as one of the best pass-protecting RTs in 2025 and helps protect Jaxson Dart. At $13M/yr he is a slight below-market bargain for an above-average starter. RT was a significant risk before this signing — now fully resolved. OT need fully eliminated.

1yr/$10M (up to $10M with escalators, only $3M guaranteed) is a high-upside prove-it deal after losing Cor'Dale Flott. Newsome is a former 2021 first-round pick (Browns, 26th overall) who had 3 elite years before declining in 2024 and being traded to Jacksonville. One-year deal with minimal guarantees screams 'bridge/competition,' not 'solved.' The Giants still clearly view CB as a priority for the 2026 draft. CB need partially addressed — Newsome could start opposite Adebo but the one-year structure means the team must still draft a long-term CB2. Need tier drops from 1 to 2.

2yr/$7.63M (max $8.5M) makes Ricard the highest-paid fullback in the NFL. This is a pure scheme-fit signing under Harbaugh — the Giants did not have an established fullback need previously. Ricard is a 6x Pro Bowler who will lead-block for Cam Skattebo and Tyrone Tracy in Harbaugh's power-run offense. Not addressing a pre-existing need, but a scheme-defining addition.

1yr/$3M depth/rotational signing. Washington is coming off a torn Achilles that cost him most of 2025. He'll be the team's third safety behind Tyler Nubin and Jevon Holland and may take some slot duties. Injury history (IR three of five seasons) limits the impact. Partially replaces Dane Belton in the depth chart. S was not a pressing need; this keeps depth adequate.

3yr/$12.3M makes Stout the NFL's highest-paid punter. 2025 first-team All-Pro (50.1 gross average, 44.9 net average — 1st in NFL). Replaces Jamie Gillan (who ranked 29th-30th in key punting metrics). Pure upgrade move at specialist; punter was not a formal team need per se but was a bottom-of-league weakness. Harbaugh made specialist investment a priority.

1yr deal (terms approximate; official figures not disclosed). Sanders is a former All-Pro who missed all of 2025 with a hip injury. Competes with Ben Sauls (8-for-8 as a rookie). Kicker was not a pre-FA team need tier target — Graham Gano was cut as a cap casualty and Sauls was the internal solution. Specialism upgrade, not a positional need fulfillment.

1yr/$1.5M base ($4.5M max with incentives, only $200K guaranteed) — a near-minimum deal. Austin brings elite speed (4.32 40-time), slot/return versatility, and 31 catches/372 yards/3 TDs in 2025. He partially addresses the Wan'Dale Robinson vacancy as a slot/return option, but the minimal guarantees and volume replacement profile (Robinson had 92 catches) make this depth-level impact. WR remains a meaningful draft need. Austin is a comp for Robinson's ST role more than his offensive production.

1yr/$3.75M re-sign (plus $2M incentives) pairs McFadden alongside Edmunds as the ILB duo. McFadden missed 2025 with a foot injury but posted 107 tackles in 2024. At $3.75M he is a cost-effective depth/starter piece. Combined with Edmunds, the LB room is addressed for 2026, though neither is considered a long-term answer. Contributes to 'fully_addressed' classification for LB alongside Edmunds.

1yr prove-it re-sign. Hodgins (10 catches/115 yards/1 TD in 7 games in 2025) is a physical receiver who fits Harbaugh's blocking scheme. One-year deal at well below starter money. Adds WR depth but does not solve the Robinson replacement need. WR remains a primary draft target.

Veteran minimum ($1.215M) re-sign of the 2022 7th-overall pick. Neal is expected to compete at guard (not tackle) where the Giants have a right side opening. His pass block win rate of 82.9% was dead last among qualifiers at tackle, but Harbaugh saw something on tape. Minimum deal = high upside/zero downside. Does not resolve the interior OL starter need but adds cheap competition.

1yr depth re-sign. Stinnie is a trusted backup guard with some center flexibility — partially mitigating the Schlottmann departure. Does not solve the starting guard need but provides quality depth insurance.

Low-cost re-sign as a slot/return specialist. Olszewski fills in at slot receiver and serves as a returner. Competes with Calvin Austin III for the punt return role. Minimal cap impact; pure depth and special teams retention.

Re-signed as the blocking TE3 behind Likely and Johnson. Manhertz's blocking contributions were an integral part of the running game. 1yr deal confirms depth role. With Likely signed, TE is now a strength; Manhertz adds specialist blocking value.
RFA re-sign primarily as a special teams contributor. Minimum-level deal. LB depth retention.
Re-signed as a rotational OLB/special teams contributor. 19 career games across three teams. Minimum deal. Does not address any starting need.
ERFA tender at league minimum. Green was the team's best punt gunner and a special teams tackle leader despite missing 5 games. Purely a special teams/depth retention.

Restructured from a $6.5M cap hit to approximately $2.55M, saving ~$3.95M in 2026 cap space. Singletary is the clear RB3 behind Cam Skattebo and Tyrone Tracy. Versatile pass-catcher who fits Harbaugh's scheme. Re-structure is cap management, not a positional need fill. RB is not a team need with Skattebo and Tracy.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Eluemunor re-signed (3yr/$39M, $25.45M gtd) locks up starting RT opposite Andrew Thomas for 3+ years. Bookend tackles are set. OT is no longer a need at any meaningful tier. Could still take a developmental tackle on Day 3 but not a draft priority.

Edmunds (3yr/$36M, $23.7M gtd) directly replaces Okereke as green-dot ILB. McFadden re-signed as partner. Immediate need resolved. However, neither is under 30 on a long-term deal — Edmunds is 27 on a 3-year deal and McFadden is on a 1-year prove-it. LB drops to tier 3 developmental: worth targeting in rounds 3-5 (Sonny Styles at #5 OAV still possible for Harbaugh's defense). Not a Day 1 need.

Isaiah Likely (3yr/$40M, elite market tier, clear starter) fills TE1 role. Theo Johnson returns as TE2 and Chris Manhertz re-signed as blocking TE3. The position group went from a need to a legitimate strength. No draft capital needed at TE unless it's a premium value fall.

Flott's departure to Tennessee created the need; Newsome's 1yr/$10M signing (only $3M gtd) partially addresses it. Newsome is a starter-capable player but the one-year structure and $3M guarantee makes this a bridge solution, not a resolution. CB remains a premium draft target — CB2 opposite Adebo is still unsettled long-term. Should target CB in rounds 1-2 regardless of Newsome.

Robinson's departure (92 catches, 1,014 yds in 2025) was not adequately replaced. Austin III (1yr/$1.5M base, minimum guarantees) and Hodgins (1yr depth) are slot/depth options but neither replicates Robinson's volume or reliability. Nabers (returning from ACL) makes WR2 the critical need. Giants must address WR in rounds 1-3 of the draft. Need remains at tier 2.

Schlottmann's departure removed backup center depth. Starting guard situation remains unclear with Greg Van Roten not returning and Jon Runyan's status uncertain. Neal (min deal, guard) and Stinnie (depth) are insufficient to call this resolved. IOL — particularly starting-caliber guard and a reliable backup center — remains a meaningful need. Target in rounds 2-4.

No meaningful EDGE signings. Kayvon Thibodeaux and Brian Burns remain the starters but neither has been extended and the team did not add pass rush depth in FA. Caleb Murphy is a minimum-level depth piece. EDGE need unchanged — still a legitimate consideration in the middle rounds of the draft.

Dexter Lawrence remains the cornerstone but the position next to him (referenced by Harbaugh himself as a priority) was not addressed in free agency. Rakeem Nunez-Roches' status is uncertain. IDL depth/starting partner for Lawrence unchanged as a need. Target in rounds 2-4.

Casey Kreiter's departure to Arizona created a new need at long-snapper for the first time in 7 seasons. The Giants will need to find a replacement via free agency, trade, or as an undrafted free agent. This is a newly created need.

Nubin and Holland are the starting safeties. Belton departed, Washington signed as depth (1yr/$3M, coming off Achilles tear). Safety depth is thin but the starters are intact. Ar'Darius Washington minimally addresses the depth hole. Need tier unchanged at 3 — could still look at a camp-level or late-round safety.

key_additions.headline: Top-3 paid TE reunites with Harbaugh as the offensive centerpiece, scheme-defining move for 12-personnel attack
Pro Bowl ILB replaces released Bobby Okereke as the green-dot defensive quarterback with 112 tackles in 2025
Starting RT locked up long-term on a relative bargain, preserving the bookend tackle duo protecting Jaxson Dart
6x Pro Bowl fullback follows Harbaugh to New York as NFL's highest-paid FB, installing power-run identity
Former 2021 1st-round pick on a prove-it deal bridges CB2 vacancy left by Cor'Dale Flott's departure
2025 1st-team All-Pro punter (50.1 gross avg) becomes NFL's highest-paid punter in specialist overhaul
Elite-speed slot/returner on near-minimum deal partially addresses Wan'Dale Robinson vacancy
Fourth former Raven to follow Harbaugh to New York; versatile DB adds secondary depth coming off Achilles

key_losses.headline: Giants let go of their best offensive weapon from 2025 (92-1,014-4 in Nabers' absence) — biggest loss of the offseason
Breakout CB2 (11 PD, 3 TFL in 2025) bolts for top-of-market deal, creating urgent secondary hole
Two-year defensive captain with 143 tackles in 2025 cut for cap savings, directly replaced by Tremaine Edmunds
TE2 departs to Tennessee; departure expected following Isaiah Likely signing making room redundant
Versatile C/G depth piece leaves, stripping Giants of their only reliable backup center under contract
Special teams leader and safety depth departs across town to division rival Jets
